title: Cornerstone Project Manifest
description: Schema for .cornerstone/manifest.yaml — the project-local registry root that declares emission style, defaults, transformations, and lifecycle conventions for a project's Cornerstone events.

apiVersion.description: API version. Bumped when the manifest schema breaks compatibility.

owner.description: Team or person who owns the events emitted from this project.

emission.description: How this project emits Cornerstone events. The schema validation pipeline is independent of producer, so any of these styles is valid.

style.description: Primary emission style. `sdk` uses cornerstone-sdk / @cerebras/cornerstone-sdk (recommended only for greenfield projects until SDK is GA-proven). `otel-direct` emits via OpenTelemetry's logs API with cornerstone.* attributes (recommended for projects already using OTEL). `raw-json` emits Cornerstone-shaped JSON to stdout/stderr/file (zero-dependency option). `mixed` allows multiple styles in the same project — instrument skill picks per file.

package.description: npm or pip package name (e.g. cornerstone-sdk, @cerebras/cornerstone-sdk).

logger_module.description: Where the project's shared logger is defined (e.g. my_app.logging).

attribute_prefix.description: Prefix for cornerstone fields when serialized as OTEL attributes (e.g. cornerstone.event.uuid).

preferred.description: Fallback when per-file inference can't decide. The instrument skill prefers the style already used in a given file; this is the tie-breaker.

uuid_strategy.description: How event UUIDs are minted for this project.

mode.description: `random` (default) uses UUID4 — globally unique but per-project. `deterministic` uses UUID5(namespace, project_name + ':' + sub_type) — same logical event yields the same UUID across forks; useful for shared upstream libraries.

namespace.description: UUID5 namespace; required when mode is 'deterministic'.

defaults.description: Values automatically applied to every event emitted from this project (the SDK reads these on Logger init; for otel-direct / raw-json producers, the instrument skill bakes them into the generated code).

transformations.description: Field-level transformations applied at emit time. The SDK applies them automatically; for otel-direct / raw-json producers, the instrument skill emits the transformation inline.

field.description: Dot-path to the field, e.g. initiating.user.user.email

transform.description: Built-in transformation. `redact` replaces value with '[REDACTED]'. Hash transforms produce a hex digest.

salt_env.description: Env var name carrying the salt; required when transform is 'hash-with-salt'.

max_length.description: Max length; required when transform is 'truncate'.

conventions.description: Project-wide rules enforced by `corner-lint registry` and the validator processor.

required_contexts.description: Context names that every event in this project must include.

forbidden_fields.description: Dot-path fields that must never be present (defensive: prevent accidental PII leaks).

severity_floor.description: Lowest severity level allowed in this project. Events at levels below this are rejected at validation.

sources.description: Glob patterns telling the skills where the application code lives. The instrument skill only activates inside these globs; the retrofit skill scans only these globs.
